topics.topic: Should the US increase border security and limit asylum claims?

responses.label: Pure Conservative (overt)
responses.text: I think there are two different issues. There is an economic migration issue which should be dealt with on a case by case basis, but it's not necessarily something that needs to stop completely. It does need to slow down though since we have been overrun in recent years. The second thing I would want to see addressed more stringently is illegal immigration. People who come here illegally shouldn't even get so much of a foot through our door without being deported immediately back across the Rio Grande or wherever they came from. topics.topic: What do you think about giving undocumented immigrants voting rights?

responses.text: It's illegal to give someone a privilege of citizenship without them being legally allowed the right. So, why would we vote for something that violates federal law and makes us all vulnerable to charges of fraud as well if this is ever implemented? I wouldn't want people who are here illegally to have any privileges or access to anything other than legalization. They should be required to go through a very lengthy process (5-10 years) before they can even apply for green card status. That seems fair enough...or at least it did until 202

responses.label: Pure politics (overt)
responses.text: The idea that illegal immigrants are taking jobs from Americans is a myth. There’s plenty of evidence showing they take the jobs nobody else wants, and employers wouldn't hire them if it weren't for their status as undocumented aliens who can be paid less because they won't complain or get fired without being deported. They also drive down wages in industries where there aren’t enough legal citizens willing to do those low paying, menial labor positions like landscaping/construction work.
